process.name: Solar Permit Workflow
permitTypes: Solar permit requests submitted through DevHub
Solar permit requests that require plan review or Single PDF Process upload rules when applicable

stages.name: Confirm solar permit type
stages.description: Confirm that the project is a solar permit request listed in public DevHub application guidance before preparing a fixture-only submittal checklist.

evidence.note: Existing public DevHub application guidance fixture identifies solar permits as a DevHub permit request type.

stages.name: Screen solar project scope
stages.description: Collect only the project facts needed to decide whether the solar work can follow this solar permit skeleton and whether plan-review-style upload rules apply.

evidence.note: DevHub permit requests are project-specific online permitting transactions.

stages.name: Prepare solar permit documents
stages.description: Prepare the solar application, drawing plans, and supporting calculations or reports as fixture-described PDFs before any DevHub upload is attempted.

evidence.note: Existing Single PDF Process fixture says drawing plans are one searchable PDF and applications, calculations, and supporting documents are separate PDFs.

stages.name: Enter DevHub solar request
stages.description: Use DevHub only as a modeled workflow step in this fixture, requiring project, applicant, property, contractor, and scope facts before upload preview.

evidence.note: Existing public guidance fixture lists solar permits among DevHub permit request types.

stages.name: Preview solar upload package
stages.description: Produce a reversible preview of the solar PDF package and file-rule checks; do not perform an official upload without exact user confirmation.

evidence.note: Existing DevHub FAQ fixture lists permit requests requiring plan review, correction uploads, and fee payments as online services.

stages.name: Review certification and submit
stages.description: Stop for user review of the solar request, uploaded-file list, and any certification language before certification or submission.

evidence.note: Submitting a DevHub permit request is an official workflow action and is modeled as a confirmation gate.

stages.name: Prescreen, intake, and fee payment checkpoints
stages.description: After submission, track PP&D intake status and any fee-payment checkpoint; payment remains a financial action requiring exact confirmation.

evidence.note: Existing DevHub FAQ fixture identifies fee payments as an online permitting service.

stages.name: Solar plan review and corrections
stages.description: Track plan review and prepare correction response PDFs if PP&D issues checksheets or correction requests; official correction upload remains confirmation-gated.

evidence.note: Existing DevHub FAQ fixture identifies correction uploads as an online permitting service.

stages.name: Approval, inspections, and closeout
stages.description: After approval or issuance, inspection scheduling and closeout actions are modeled as user-confirmed workflow steps, not automated fixture behavior.

evidence.note: Existing DevHub FAQ fixture lists inspection scheduling for online trade permits among service categories; this solar skeleton treats any scheduling action as confirmation-gated until a solar-specific fixture is added.

requiredFacts.label: Solar project property address or property identifier
requiredFacts.description: The solar permit request needs the project location before DevHub request preparation.
requiredFacts.validationHint: Ask for the project address or a public property identifier.

evidence.note: Permit requests are project-specific DevHub transactions.

requiredFacts.label: Solar project scope and equipment summary
requiredFacts.description: The fixture needs a concise description of the proposed solar work to keep the permit type and document checklist aligned.
requiredFacts.validationHint: Ask whether the work is solar PV, solar thermal, battery-related, roof-mounted, ground-mounted, new, replacement, or revision work when known.

evidence.note: Existing public guidance fixture distinguishes solar permits as a selectable request type.

requiredFacts.label: Applicant contact information
requiredFacts.description: A DevHub permit request needs an applicant or account contact for the project transaction.
requiredFacts.validationHint: Ask for applicant identity only in redacted or user-provided form; never place private values in fixtures.

evidence.note: DevHub is the online system for permit request transactions.

requiredFacts.label: Contractor or installer identity and license facts when applicable
requiredFacts.description: The fixture treats installer and license facts as required planning facts until solar-specific public fixture extraction narrows the conditions.
requiredFacts.validationHint: Ask for contractor or installer information only when applicable and keep private values out of committed fixtures.

evidence.note: Existing DevHub fixture supports project-specific permit request data entry; contractor specifics require later solar extraction.

requiredFacts.label: Solar project valuation when requested
requiredFacts.description: Valuation is tracked as a missing fact because fees and review routing may depend on project-specific permit request data.
requiredFacts.validationHint: Ask for valuation only when required by the selected DevHub fixture state or later solar guidance extraction.

evidence.note: Existing DevHub FAQ fixture includes fee payment as an online permitting service.

requiredDocuments.name: Solar permit application PDF
requiredDocuments.description: Application material is modeled as a separate PDF from drawing plans under the existing Single PDF Process fixture rules.

evidence.note: Existing Single PDF Process fixture treats applications as separate PDFs from drawing plans.

requiredDocuments.name: Solar drawing plan PDF
requiredDocuments.description: Solar plans are modeled as one searchable drawing-plan PDF when Single PDF Process upload rules apply.

evidence.note: Existing Single PDF Process fixture says drawing plans are one searchable PDF.

requiredDocuments.name: Solar structural, electrical, or equipment supporting PDFs
requiredDocuments.description: Calculations, equipment details, reports, and other supporting solar documents are modeled as separate PDFs when required by the selected solar scope.

evidence.note: Existing Single PDF Process fixture says calculations and supporting documents are separate PDFs.

requiredDocuments.name: Solar correction response PDF
requiredDocuments.description: If PP&D issues checksheets or corrections, the response package is modeled as a separate PDF and remains official-upload gated.

evidence.note: Existing DevHub FAQ fixture identifies correction uploads as an online permitting service.

requiredDocuments.name: Solar fee payment notice or invoice
requiredDocuments.description: Fee notices or payment prompts are tracked as financial checkpoints rather than files an agent may pay automatically.

evidence.note: Existing DevHub FAQ fixture identifies fee payments as an online permitting service.

fileRules.description: Solar drawing plans are represented as one searchable PDF when plan-review or Single PDF Process rules apply.

evidence.note: Existing Single PDF Process fixture says drawing plans are one searchable PDF.

fileRules.description: Solar applications, calculations, reports, equipment documents, and correction responses stay separate from the drawing-plan PDF.

evidence.note: Existing Single PDF Process fixture says applications, calculations, and supporting documents are separate PDFs.

fileRules.description: The fixture permits checklist and preview work only; official upload, certification, submission, correction upload, inspection scheduling, and payment actions require exact user confirmation.

evidence.note: Existing DevHub FAQ fixture describes official online permit services including permit requests, corrections, fee payments, and inspections.

actionGates.name: Solar official upload gate
actionGates.description: Uploading solar documents to an official DevHub request may affect the permit record and requires exact user confirmation.
actionGates.requiredConfirmation: User explicitly confirms uploading these named solar permit files to this DevHub request.

evidence.note: Existing DevHub FAQ fixture lists permit request and correction upload services.

actionGates.name: Solar certification gate
actionGates.description: Any certification or acknowledgment text must be reviewed and confirmed by the user before proceeding.
actionGates.requiredConfirmation: User explicitly confirms the certification or acknowledgment text for this solar permit request.

evidence.note: Existing DevHub application fixture models permit request submission as an official workflow action.

actionGates.name: Solar submission gate
actionGates.description: The agent must stop before submitting the solar permit request unless the user confirms this exact submission.
actionGates.requiredConfirmation: User explicitly confirms submitting this completed solar permit request to PP&D.

evidence.note: Existing DevHub guidance fixture identifies DevHub as the online permitting system for permit requests.

actionGates.name: Solar fee payment gate
actionGates.description: Any fee payment prompt is a financial action and cannot be executed from the fixture without exact user confirmation.
actionGates.requiredConfirmation: User explicitly confirms paying the displayed PP&D solar permit fee amount for this permit request in this session.

evidence.note: Existing DevHub FAQ fixture lists fee payments as an online permitting service.

actionGates.name: Solar correction upload gate
actionGates.description: Uploading solar correction responses is an official correction action and requires exact user confirmation.
actionGates.requiredConfirmation: User explicitly confirms uploading these named solar correction response files to this permit request.

evidence.note: Existing DevHub FAQ fixture identifies correction uploads as an online permitting service.

actionGates.name: Solar inspection scheduling gate
actionGates.description: Inspection scheduling is treated as consequential until a later solar-specific fixture narrows the exact workflow.
actionGates.requiredConfirmation: User explicitly confirms scheduling the selected inspection for this solar permit request.

evidence.note: Existing DevHub FAQ fixture lists inspection scheduling for online trade permits among service categories; this fixture fails closed for solar until later extraction.
